Title: ZEITLUPE Promotes ABA-Induced Stomatal Closure in Arabidopsis and Populus

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Loss-of-function ztl mutants have reduced ABA-induced stomatal closure, higher stomatal conductance and increased water loss compared with WT, without changes in stomatal density.
2. Overexpression of ZTL (p35S::HA::ZTL) in the ztl-3 background rescues the elevated water loss phenotype, confirming ZTL’s role in stomatal regulation.
3. ZTL is required to sustain ABA-induced expression of key ABA-signalling (e.g. OST1, ABI5, ABF4) and ABA-responsive (RD29A, RAB18) genes.
4. ZTL physically interacts with OST1 but does not promote OST1 degradation, and ZTL acts synergistically with OST1 and antagonistically with PRR5 to integrate circadian clock outputs with ABA signalling to control stomatal aperture.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZTL positively regulates ABA-induced stomatal closure and water conservation; loss-of-function ztl mutants show reduced ABA sensitivity of stomata, increased stomatal conductance and leaf water loss, and this phenotype is rescued by ZTL overexpression; ZTL is required to sustain ABA-induced expression of key ABA-signalling genes and acts synergistically with OST1.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: ZTL is a blue light photoreceptor and F-box protein that functions in an E3 ubiquitin ligase complex; it post-translationally regulates clock proteins such as PRR5 and TOC1 by promoting their proteasomal degradation and physically interacts with OST1 without affecting OST1 protein stability; ZTL is also required at the transcriptional level to sustain ABA-induced expression of several ABA-signalling and ABA-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Circadian clock signalling pathway and ABA signalling pathway controlling guard-cell ion transport and stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ZTL acts upstream of multiple ABA-signalling components by sustaining ABA-induced transcription of OST1, ABI5, ABF4, RD29A and RAB18; genetic and physiological data indicate that ZTL and OST1 act synergistically to promote ABA-induced stomatal closure; ZTL acts upstream of and post-translationally regulates the clock substrate PRR5, and PRR5 in turn modulates ABA signalling upstream of OST1.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box clock protein and blue light photoreceptor with an N-terminal flavin-binding LOV (Light, Oxygen or Voltage) domain and Kelch repeats, acting in an E3-ligase complex.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Interacts physically with OST1 in Arabidopsis protoplasts (co-immunoprecipitation); interaction with TOC1 (used as a positive control, consistent with ZTL targeting TOC1 for degradation).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: ZTL protein abundance shows a daily rhythm with high levels in the late afternoon/evening (around ZT 8–9), and ZTL function is analysed in leaves/guard cells at this time when ABA sensitivity is high; ZTL is required to sustain ABA-induced transcription of several ABA-signalling genes (e.g. OST1, ABI5, ABF4, RD29A, RAB18).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA application (10 μM) for stomatal assays and gene expression; detached-leaf desiccation as a proxy for drought; growth under controlled long-day light:dark and temperature cycles; CO2-free aeration for stomatal aperture measurements.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Integration of circadian clock signalling with ABA-dependent stomatal closure, water loss, and drought-related stress responses
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (ABA-induced stomatal closure, drought-related water loss); stomatal conductance / water use efficiency; circadian regulation of stomatal movements
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Stomatal density is unchanged in ztl-3 compared with WT (208 vs 210 stomata/mm²), indicating increased water loss arises from increased stomatal openness; detached-leaf water loss and stomatal conductance are significantly higher in ztl-3 than WT across multiple time points (p<0.05 to p<0.001).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: ztl-3 mutants show a significant delay in radicle emergence (seed germination) compared to WT; ztl-3 mutants also display circadian defects that necessitated adjusting ABA treatment to circadian time in stomatal assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana ecotypes Col-0, C24 and Ws-2; ztl-1, ztl-3, ztl-21 single mutants; ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutant; ztl-3 prr5-1 ost1-3 triple mutant; ztl-3 plants complemented with p35S::HA::ZTL; Arabidopsis Col-0 cell suspension cultures and leaf mesophyll protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Primarily Col-0 background (ztl-3, ost1-3, prr5-1); additional alleles in C24 (ztl-1) and Ws-2 (ztl-21) backgrounds used for some assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Stomatal aperture assays in epidermal strips ±10 μM ABA under CO2-free aeration at ZT 8–9; steady-state stomatal conductance measurements with a leaf porometer; detached-leaf water loss assays; radicle emergence (seed germination) assays; generation and analysis of T-DNA insertion mutants and transgenic complementation lines; RT-qPCR of ABA signalling and responsive genes after ABA spray; co-immunoprecipitation of tagged proteins in protoplasts; protein stability assays with cycloheximide and proteasome inhibitor MG132.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse-genetic analysis of loss-of-function ztl alleles (ztl-1, ztl-3, ztl-21), double and triple mutants with ost1-3 and prr5-1; physiological assays of stomatal aperture, stomatal conductance and detached-leaf water loss; radicle emergence assays; RT-qPCR of ABA-signalling and ABA-responsive genes; complementation of ztl-3 with p35S::HA::ZTL; co-immunoprecipitation of ZTL with OST1 in Arabidopsis protoplasts; protein stability assays in protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics using existing T-DNA insertion alleles ztl-1, ztl-3 and ztl-21; cloning of ZTL coding sequence into a CaMV35S-driven p35S::HA::ZTL construct for overexpression and complementation; cloning of ZTL cDNA into epitope-tagged expression vectors for protoplast assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because ZTL is required for efficient ABA-induced stomatal closure, limits water loss without altering stomatal density, and its role is conserved in Populus, modulating ZTL activity offers a potential strategy to improve water use efficiency and drought tolerance by aligning stomatal behaviour with the circadian clock in crops and trees.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ost1-3 mutants have elevated stomatal conductance and detached-leaf water loss compared with WT, confirming OST1 as a key positive regulator of ABA-induced stomatal closure.
2. The stomatal and water loss phenotypes of ztl-3 resemble those of ost1-3, and the ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutant shows an even stronger water loss phenotype, indicating synergistic action of OST1 and ZTL.
3. OST1 physically interacts with both ZTL and PRR5 in plant cells, integrating ABA kinase signalling with circadian clock components.
4. OST1 protein stability is not detectably affected by ZTL or proteasome inhibition in protoplasts, suggesting that ZTL regulates ABA signalling primarily via mechanisms other than OST1 degradation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OST1 acts as a central positive regulator of ABA-induced stomatal closure; ost1-3 mutants show reduced stomatal closure in response to ABA, elevated stomatal conductance, and higher detached-leaf water loss compared with WT; in combination with ztl-3, loss of OST1 produces an even stronger water loss phenotype, indicating that OST1 and ZTL act synergistically to control stomatal aperture.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Serine/threonine SnRK2 protein kinase (SnRK2.6/SRK2E) that is released from PP2C inhibition upon ABA perception and autophosphorylation; phosphorylates targets to inhibit K+ influx and activate S-type anion channels to drive guard-cell depolarisation and stomatal closure; interacts physically with ZTL and PRR5, but its protein stability is not altered by ZTL in protoplast assays.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Core ABA signal transduction pathway controlling guard-cell ion channels and stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OST1 functions downstream of ABA receptors and PP2Cs in the ABA core signalling module and upstream of ion channels controlling stomatal aperture; genetic interactions show that ZTL and OST1 together promote stomatal closure, and PRR5 likely acts upstream of OST1 to inhibit ABA signalling, as loss of PRR5 does not alter the ost1-3 water loss phenotype but modulates the ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutant.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Physically interacts with ZTL and with PRR5 in Arabidopsis protoplasts (co-immunoprecipitation).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OST1 transcript levels are induced by ABA treatment in WT Arabidopsis, but this ABA-induced expression is significantly reduced in the ztl-3 background.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA (10 μM) for stomatal assays and gene expression; desiccation of detached leaves to mimic drought; controlled long-day light and temperature regimes.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of the ABA-activated kinase OST1 in stomatal closure, water loss, and its integration with circadian clock components ZTL and PRR5.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (ABA-induced stomatal closure, drought-related water loss); stomatal conductance / water use efficiency
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Detached-leaf water loss and stomatal conductance are significantly higher in ost1-3 than WT at all examined time points (p<0.05 to p<0.001), with water loss phenotypes comparable to ztl-3 and most severe in the ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutant.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: ost1-3 mutants show a significant delay in radicle emergence (seed germination) similar to ztl-3, indicating shared roles in an ABA-sensitive developmental phase transition.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 ost1-3 T-DNA mutant; ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutant; ztl-3 prr5-1 ost1-3 triple mutant; protoplasts transiently expressing HA-OST1 and Myc-OST1; Arabidopsis Col-0 suspension cells for protoplast assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Col-0 background (ost1-3 single mutant and combinations with ztl-3 and prr5-1).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Steady-state stomatal conductance measurements in intact leaves; detached-leaf water loss assays; stomatal aperture measurements under ABA treatment; generation and analysis of single, double and triple mutants involving ost1-3; co-immunoprecipitation in protoplasts to test OST1 interactions with ZTL and PRR5; protein stability assays with cycloheximide and MG132.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of the strong loss-of-function ost1-3 T-DNA mutant; physiological measurements of stomatal conductance and detached-leaf water loss in ost1-3 and in double/triple mutants with ztl-3 and prr5-1; comparison of ABA-induced stomatal closure phenotypes; co-immunoprecipitation of OST1 with ZTL and PRR5 in Arabidopsis protoplasts; protein stability assays of OST1 with or without ZTL and proteasome inhibition.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics using the existing T-DNA insertion allele ost1-3; cloning of full-length OST1 cDNA into epitope-tagged pRT104 expression vectors for transient expression in protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Given OST1’s central role in ABA-induced stomatal closure and its synergistic interaction with the clock protein ZTL, manipulating OST1 activity or its upstream clock regulators has potential to enhance drought tolerance and water use efficiency in crops by strengthening ABA-responsive stomatal closure while maintaining coordination with daily environmental cycles.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PRR5 loss-of-function alleles alter stomatal ABA responses: prr5-11 stomata close more readily in response to ABA than WT, whereas prr5-1 stomata are more open than WT without ABA.
2. PRR5 physically interacts with OST1 in plant cells.
3. In water loss assays, prr5-1 single mutants resemble WT, but removing PRR5 in a ztl-3 ost1-3 background partially suppresses the severe water loss phenotype of the double mutant.
4. Genetic and biochemical evidence indicates that PRR5 acts downstream of ZTL but upstream of OST1 as an inhibitor of ABA-signalling, integrating circadian clock outputs into stomatal regulation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PRR5 modulates ABA-induced stomatal closure and water loss; prr5-11 mutants show enhanced ABA-induced stomatal closure compared with WT, while prr5-1 mutants have more open stomata than WT in the absence of ABA; loss of PRR5 partially suppresses the extreme water loss phenotype of the ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutant, indicating that PRR5 inhibits ABA-signalling downstream of ZTL but upstream of OST1.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PRR5 is a pseudo-response regulator clock protein whose abundance is controlled post-translationally by ZTL; in this study PRR5 physically interacts with OST1, and genetic data indicate that PRR5 inhibits ABA-induced stomatal closure, acting downstream of ZTL but upstream of OST1.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Circadian clock signalling pathway intersecting with ABA signalling that controls stomatal movements.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Genetic interactions indicate that PRR5 inhibits ABA-signalling controlling stomatal aperture: loss of PRR5 enhances ABA-induced stomatal closure and partially suppresses the extreme water loss phenotype of ztl-3 ost1-3; the prr5-1 ost1-3 double mutant behaves like ost1-3, supporting a model in which PRR5 acts downstream of ZTL but upstream of OST1.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PSEUDO-RESPONSE REGULATOR (PRR) family circadian clock protein.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Physically interacts with OST1 in Arabidopsis protoplasts (co-immunoprecipitation).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: PRR5 is an evening-expressed circadian clock protein; PRR proteins as a family are expressed sequentially from dawn to dusk.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA treatment with timing adjusted to circadian period; desiccation of detached leaves; controlled long-day light and temperature regimes.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of the circadian clock protein PRR5 in ABA-dependent stomatal regulation and its interaction with ZTL and OST1.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (ABA-induced stomatal closure, drought-related water loss); circadian regulation of stomatal behaviour
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: prr5-11 mutants show significantly greater ABA-induced stomatal closure than WT (p<0.001); prr5-1 single mutants display water loss rates similar to WT, whereas triple mutants lacking ZTL, PRR5 and OST1 have significantly lower water loss than ztl-3 ost1-3 double mutants between 90–180 min.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In the absence of exogenous ABA, prr5-1 mutants have more open stomata than WT, suggesting PRR5 contributes to basal stomatal regulation even without added ABA.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana prr5-11 mutant; prr5-1 T-DNA insertion mutant; ztl-3 prr5-1 double mutant; ztl-3 prr5-1 ost1-3 triple mutant; protoplasts transiently expressing tagged PRR5 and OST1.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Col-0 background (prr5-1) and prr5-11 allele in its reported background.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Stomatal aperture assays under ABA treatment with treatment time adjusted to circadian time for each genotype; detached-leaf water loss assays in single, double and triple mutants; co-immunoprecipitation of PRR5 with OST1 in protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of prr5-11 and prr5-1 loss-of-function mutants in stomatal aperture assays under ABA treatment with timing adjusted for circadian period; detached-leaf water loss assays in prr5-1 single mutants and in double/triple mutants with ztl-3 and ost1-3; co-immunoprecipitation demonstrating physical interaction between PRR5 and OST1 in protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics using existing loss-of-function alleles prr5-11 and prr5-1; cloning of full-length PRR5 cDNA into epitope-tagged pRT104 expression vectors for co-immunoprecipitation assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because PRR5 modulates ABA-induced stomatal closure and interacts with OST1, altering PRR5 activity provides a means to fine-tune the balance between water loss and stomatal closure in coordination with the circadian clock, potentially allowing breeders to adjust drought responses and growth–stress trade-offs in crops.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PttZTL1,2 expression is rhythmic in WT Populus leaves and is significantly reduced in PttZTL1,2 RNAi lines.
2. Downregulation of PttZTL1,2 lengthens the free-running circadian period in Populus leaves while maintaining strong rhythmicity.
3. PttZTL1,2 RNAi lines show reduced ABA-induced stomatal closure, higher stomatal conductance and increased detached-leaf water loss compared with WT, largely without major changes in stomatal density.
4. These phenotypes mirror those of Arabidopsis ztl mutants, demonstrating that ZTL-dependent regulation of circadian clock function and stomatal ABA responses is conserved between Arabidopsis and Populus.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi-mediated downregulation of PttZTL1 and PttZTL2 lengthens the free-running circadian period in Populus leaves and reduces ABA-induced stomatal closure, leading to increased stomatal conductance and higher detached-leaf water loss, largely without changes in stomatal density; these phenotypes mirror Arabidopsis ztl mutants, indicating conserved ZTL function in Populus.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PttZTL1 and PttZTL2 act as Populus ZTL homologues required to maintain normal circadian period and to promote ABA-induced stomatal closure; reduced PttZTL1,2 expression slows the circadian clock and decreases stomatal responsiveness to ABA, increasing stomatal conductance and water loss.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Circadian clock pathway and ABA signalling pathway controlling stomatal movements and water loss in Populus.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Downregulation of PttZTL1,2 slows the Populus circadian clock and diminishes ABA-induced stomatal closure, functionally paralleling the role of ZTL upstream of ABA-responsive stomatal regulation described in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ZTL homologues (F-box blue light photoreceptor with LOV domain) in Populus.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: PttZTL expression is rhythmic in WT Populus leaves and is downregulated to approximately 40% of WT levels at ZT 8–9 in PttZTL1,2 RNAi lines 5 and 7.
Plant_Genes.Species: Hybrid aspen (Populus tremula L. × P. tremuloides Michx.) cv. T89
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth under long-day photoperiods (LD 18:6 then 15:9) at 18–20°C; exogenous ABA treatment in stomatal assays; detached-leaf desiccation to mimic drought.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Conserved function of Populus ZTL homologues (PttZTL1 and PttZTL2) in circadian clock regulation and ABA-dependent stomatal control and water loss.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Circadian rhythm period; abiotic stress (ABA-induced stomatal closure, drought-related water loss); stomatal conductance
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In delayed fluorescence assays, WT Populus leaves have a free-running period of ~21.1 h, whereas PttZTL1,2 RNAi line 5 shows ~29.2 h and line 7 ~25.4 h; in stomatal density measurements, RNAi line 5 has 135 stomata/mm² compared to 141 stomata/mm² in WT, while line 7 does not differ significantly from WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: PttZTL1,2 RNAi line 5 shows earlier growth cessation than WT; both RNAi lines display longer circadian periods but remain rhythmic, indicating a slower-running yet functional clock.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus tremula × P. tremuloides cv. T89 WT trees; PttZTL1,2 RNAi lines (10 independent lines generated, with lines 5 and 7 selected for detailed analysis).
Plant_Genes.Variety: Hybrid aspen Populus tremula × P. tremuloides cv. T89.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of Populus with a PttZTL1,2 RNAi construct; growth of WT and RNAi trees under controlled long-day conditions; RT-qPCR to quantify PttZTL1,2 expression; delayed fluorescence imaging of detached leaves to estimate circadian period; stomatal aperture assays under ABA treatment; stomatal conductance measurements in intact leaves; detached-leaf water loss assays; stomatal density measurements on abaxial leaf surfaces.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation of transgenic Populus tremula × P. tremuloides cv. T89 PttZTL1,2 RNAi lines; RT-qPCR to confirm downregulation of PttZTL1 and PttZTL2 transcripts; delayed fluorescence imaging to measure circadian period; stomatal aperture assays under ABA treatment; stomatal conductance measurements; detached-leaf water loss assays; stomatal density measurements.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics via RNA interference: a PttZTL fragment amplified from Populus cDNA was cloned into pDONOR201 and recombined into the pHELLSGATE8 binary vector to generate a construct targeting both PttZTL1 and PttZTL2; transgenic RNAi lines were produced by Agrobacterium-mediated transformation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because reduced PttZTL1,2 expression lengthens circadian period and increases stomatal conductance and water loss in Populus, while ZTL function is conserved with Arabidopsis, manipulating ZTL activity in trees could be exploited to adjust stomatal behaviour, improve water use efficiency and enhance drought tolerance in forestry breeding programs.
